author: 77Scouser
text: Liverpool 2026/27 Season - Iraola Officially Confirmed2 hours agovia mobileReplyQuoteSelect PostDeselect PostLink to PostMemberGive GiftBack to TopPost by 77Scouser on2 hours ago17 hours agoMysteronsaid:20 hours agoClem77said:I've never been coached in football and don't have a clue how hard it is to implement a style of football that differs from what players have been used to. So for all those more tactically astute than myself, what are the expectations for those first 5 league games?Last time outNewcastle (A) - we won 3-2 but that does not tell the story... We raced into a 2-0 lead and then like in many games last season.. we took our foot off the gas and they levelled to make it 2-2..Even Gordon being sent off just before halftime changed nothing, they cut through us time and again.... It was a feisty gameNgumoha scored the winner in the 10th minute of time added on (90, +10)Nottm Forest (H) - Conceded early - heads dropped and conceded some more... This game was that bad that fans left early from Anfield... Crisis has morphed into catastrophe with no signs of slowing in this game, it was the 7th defeat on the trot in the premier league... We lost this game 3-0 at AnfieldIpswich (A) - Two seasons ago we kicked of the campaign at Ipswich - Slots 1st competitive game.. Ipswich started better and worried our defence, we gave a lot of fouls away, but scored two goals after the 60th minute to win 2-0Fulham (H) - We won this game 2-0 - but it is remembered more as the win after losing to brighton, man city and psg. This was the game of 5 changes to the team from the last defeat... But this game was not without incident, we scored both goals in the 1st half, but we were nervous in the 2nd and never pushed on...Bournemouth (A) - We lost this 3-2 - Iraola will know  all about this one - This was our good game, bad game phase... And this bad game was between 2 wins in the Champions League against Marseille and Qarabag... Bournemouth got the winner with the last kick of the game in the 95th minuteSo we won 3 and lost 2Wow perhaps it's age but I can't recall much about those games except the Newcastle one with Rio's late winner, I would prefer to believe that I have rid myself of the memories of some of the most boring and passive football in many years.I'm so much more optimistic about this season under Iraola that I don't expect to lose any of our opening games listed, in fact I fancy a 5 match winning start to have us setting the pace for the season.
